Michael Mengarelli of the Kelly Law Group (650.591.2282) for plaintiffs and responding parties; Milla L. Lvovich and Anne Yarovoy of Lvovich & Volchegursky LLP (415.392.2560) for defendant and moving party

Argued and the Court adopts its tentative ruling as follows: Defendant's motion to tax costs sought by Evgeni Litvak is granted in part and denied in part. Mr. Litvak is the prevailing party in his litigation with defendant, having won a net damage award in the homeowners association portion of the case and having achieved his objective in the partition portion of the case. Costs are taxed in the amount of $679.67, representing the $25 fee included in item 1 and the interpreter's fee in item 13. The Court does not apportion the other costs because the other plaintiffs had fee waivers, so Mr. Litvak incurred the full amount of the costs. The remainder of the costs are allowed. Moving party to submit a proposed form of order. Judge: Peter J. Busch, Clerk: E. Larnauti, Not Reported (504)

Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn To Strike/Alt, Tax Costs
Off Calendar May-03-2013 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: PLAINTIFFS' MOTION TO STRIKE, OR IN THE ALTERNATIVE, TAX COSTS

Appearances:
Michael Mengarelli of the Kelly Law Group (650.591.2282) for plaintiffs and moving parties; Milla L. Lvovich and Anne Yarovoy of Lvovich & Volchegursky LLP (415.392.2560) for defendant and responding party

Argued and the Court modifies its ruling as follows: granted in part and denied in part. Defendant is the prevailing party in the portion of the action brought by Zinoviy Litvak and Raisa Galanter concerning their claim of an outstanding loan. He is not the prevailing party in the balance of the case. Evgeni Litvak prevailed against him for the reasons set out in the ruling on the motion to tax costs he sought. Defendant brought Samarina into the case and failed to win a net monetary recovery against her. Although the jury found she breached a fiduciary duty owed to defendant, any recovery for that will only be whatever rent allocations he would have gotten anyway in the accounting. The jury verdict will not augment that amount, and that amount will be set off against the other items the referee will take into account. His only hope of getting additional money was proving his theory of waste, which the jury rejected. It is unlikely defendant will obtain a net recovery, but, in any event, any determination that he will get one is premature. Mere defense of Samarina's cross-claims does not make defendant a prevailing party, given his status as to her of practical plaintiff. Therefore, the Court has examined the claimed costs, allowed 100% where the costs relate specifically to the loan claim, 50% where the costs relate to all claims, and none where the costs relate only to claims other than the loan claim or the Court concludes that the costs are not appropriate to assess. Applying these principles, item 1 is taxed in the amount of $300 by eliminating the $40 fee related to the property sale motion and splitting the balance. Item 2 is taxed in the amount of $473. Item 4 is taxed in the amount of $7,181.60, eliminating costs related to the tenants and banks and the cancelled deposition, allowing all of the costs related to Yermash and Victoria Galanter, and splitting the balance. Item 5 is taxed in the amount of $1506.45, splitting Samarina, allowing all of Galanter and Yermash, and eliminating the balance. Item 11 is taxed in the amount of $238.93. Item 12 is taxed in the amount of $4064. Item 13 is taxed in the amount of $2539.43 (eliminating $386 and splitting the balance). The total amount taxed is $16,303.41. The balance is allowed. Moving party to submit a proposed form of order. Judge: Peter J. Busch, Clerk: E. Larnauti, Not Reported (504)

Mtn For Atty'S Fees
Off Calendar May-03-2013 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANT YURI GALASHOV'S MOTION FOR ATTORNEY'S FEES

Appearances:
Michael Mengarelli of the Kelly Law Group (650.591.2282) for plaintiffs and responding parties; Milla L. Lvovich and Anne Yarovoy of Lvovich & Volchegursky LLP (415.392.2560) for defendant and moving party

Argued and the Court adopts its tentative ruling as follows: Defendant's motion for an award of attorneys fees is denied. As a practical matter, defendant did not prevail on the portion of the case related to the homeowner association account. Although Samarina was unsuccessful in proving her case against him, the jury found that he wrongfully took the money in the account. As to this portion of the case he did not succeed in his objective of establishing his right to the account and, in fact, was determined to be a wrongdoer who converted the money in the account. It would be wholly unreasonable to conclude that he prevailed and should be awarded fees given that outcome. In any event, the request for over $270,000 is grossly overreaching and would independently justify denying the motion. The homeowner association account issue was a dispute over about $17,000. It was a very small part of the case as a whole. It in no way justified the expenditure of more than a quarter of a million dollars. Defendant seeks to recover fees for work on different aspects of the case without properly apportioning, and apportionment would be essential, especially given the disproportion of the total to the amount at stake in the claims that could support a fee award. Plaintiff to submit a proposed form of order. Judge: Peter J. Busch, Clerk: E. Larnauti, Not Reported (504)
